title: Yakuza 0
notes:
Great performance. Has to be started 1st with the steam-runtime (native runtime off). To get the steam controller working, the hid_steam kernel driver has to be blacklisted.
The special move works, everything's good.
It runs flawlessly but it always crashes in the fight tutorial when throwing a special attack. so now i have to live in that small chunk of street before the tutorial for the rest of my life.
title: Yakuza 0

notes:
Run in borderless window. Only a mild performance hit compared to native. <25% @4k
Noticed a single extremely minor graphical glitch in that flames from flamethrowers don't appear. Might be a port issue for all I know.

gpu: X.Org Radeon RX 580 Series (POLARIS10, DRM 3.26.0, 4.18.7-1-default, LLVM 6.0.1)
notes: The game runs fine, but as soon as the first boss cutscene appears, the gpu freezes. There is a save file right after the boss fight, however it crashes in certain parts of Sotenbori (though this is apparently a problem with all RX series cards). And finally, you can't progress throught the story after chapter 13 due to a cutscene freezing the gpu, again.

notes: No Steam Controller support. As with Windows 10, requires vsync or there is incredible amounts of tearing. Must be played on lowest settings to get any kind of playable framerates (14fps average).
title: Yakuza 0

notes: Game will close immediately if set to windowed mode. If you are in fullscreen mode and tab out, the game will freeze (probably due to not being able to tell you've tabbed back in). Changing the display mode will cause the same problem, but not if you run the game again to set windowed mode.
title: Yakuza 0

cpu: AMD A8-7600 Radeon R7, 10 Compute Cores 4C+6G
notes: Game crashes with the heavy punch.
title: Yakuza 0

notes:
Same as previous reports, game crashes when trying to heavy punch. Also, despite running at 1080p with a GTX1070, the framerate stutters even though both in-game FPS counter and Steam FPS counter show 60fps or above. I'd say the game feels like it's running at 45-50ish fps.

No tweaks tried yet.
title: Yakuza 0

notes: Ran on an NTFS Samsung 850 EVO SSD. Full Steam Controller support since Kernel 4.19.4. Game needs a while to "warm up," runs at 80+ FPS on high settings with vsync off after you've loaded into a save for about 30 seconds. A similar kind of treatment is needed on Windows 10. With vsync on, performance takes a decent hit, only running at around 45fps. The only issue I encountered was no sound effect at the "SEGA" splash screen.
title: Yakuza 0

notes: Performance is absolutely perfect. Has some performance issues if Plasma dies. You need a small path (ideally with no spaces or special characters) for the arcade games to work. Audio does not work in arcade games. Do not set the game to Borderless Fullscreen, as you will not be able to change that setting without the game crashing. I recommend playing without vsync for best performance.
title: Yakuza 0

notes:
After disconnecting every other controller, Big Picture with DS4 controller worked perfectly fine. The only issue I currently have are related to sound:
No sound for: SEGA logo (negligible) and arcade minigames (slightly annoying).
Also, after about +2 hours ingame, all sound sources seem to double/echo. Fixed by restarting the entire game.
title: Yakuza 0

notes: Game isn't playable with Proton 3.16-6 beta. Launching the game reaches black screen with Yakuza logo but immediately closes.
title: Yakuza 0

notes: Shows the black Yakuza 0 logo and then crashes on every available beta for Proton.
title: Yakuza 0

notes: Runs flawless. For the people that are having problems with the tutorial strong punch crash, you have to move the game to another disk or installation folder and then change the steam library path to it. This is not a WINE problem but a bug in the game itself. Windows users are also affected but it varies from system to system.
